arts.ttl: Gasperini: “Shakhtar Donetsk may not be Arsenal, but they’re still a valuable team”
arts.sum: Atalanta manager Gian Piero Gasperini spoke to the media ahead of his team’s UEFA Champions League match against Shakhtar Donetsk. The Italian coach expressed confidence in his squad’s form following the international break, despite a recent loss to Como. “We’ve started well after the transfer window closed,” Gasperini stated. “We had that defeat against Como, but the team put in a good performance in Bologna. There are many new players, and we’re integrating everyone progressively.” Regarding the upcoming match against Shakhtar Donetsk, Gasperini emphasized the quality of the opposition: “This is a group of eight matches, all of which are The post Gasperini: “Shakhtar Donetsk may not be Arsenal, but they’re still a valuable team” appeared first on FootItalia.com.

arts.ttl: Arsenal may have already found next Ethan Nwaneri as wonderkid sends strong message
arts.sum: After coming off the bench in the North London derby win over Tottenham, the 17-year-old scored his first senior goals against Bolton Wanderers in the League Cup last week before helping them defeat Leicester City at the weekend. The 16-year-old was making his debut in the competition after scoring five goals in four Premier League U18 fixtures this season - including a hat-trick at Brighton at the weekend.

arts.ttl: PSG ultras dressed in black descend on London ahead of Champions League clash with Arsenal as diehard fans are spotted lighting flares before huge game
arts.sum: Hours before the game a sizeable group of travelling supporters were filmed massed in Highbury Fields, a short walk from the Emirates Stadium.

arts.ttl: Why Ousmane Dembele isn't playing for PSG against Arsenal in Champions League game
arts.sum: The match [against Arsenal] is very important and I want all my players to be ready, so as a consequence I have left him [Dembele] out. Enrique continued: "It doesn't mean this is irreversible, but I made the best decision for the team, and that's why I signed here, to create a team that has a strong identity and has a lot of character.

arts.ttl: Latest Arsenal Team News, Injuries and Suspensions
arts.sum: Martin Odegaard's injury in September was a significant setback for the Gunners.

arts.ttl: Arsenal receive brand new Leandro Trossard red card update after huge Michael Oliver decision
arts.sum: In the late stages of the first half, the Belgium international was shown a second yellow card for delaying the restart after picking up his first caution earlier in the clash for a cynical foul. William Saliba was also cautioned for preventing Tottenham restarting the action during the North London Derby, and now Michael Oliver, the same official who sent off Trossard, has continued his strict stance on kicking the ball away.

arts.ttl: Michael Oliver's Arsenal refereeing record and huge Man City difference as he's left off list
arts.sum: Research compiled by Bonus Code Bets shows that, prior to the most recent round of matches, Oliver issued more red cards to Arsenal players than any other team in the Premier League. Additionally, stats show an inclination to brandish yellow cards to Arsenal players at a rate above the norm, with the the Gunners averaging 1.77 bookings each game under Oliver's watch, as opposed to the typical 1.63 for all top-league encounters Oliver has refereed.

arts.ttl: Mikel Arteta has finally found the Arsenal star to unleash Gabriel Martinelli
arts.sum: Injuries have seen Martinelli start the last three Premier League games with his first assist of the season arriving against Manchester City. On social media following the game, summer signing Riccardo Calafiori, who has started the last two Premier League games, was praised heavily for helping Martinelli go back to what he does best.

arts.ttl: 10 players set to miss Arsenal vs PSG as Champions League clash loses biggest stars
arts.sum: The duo are two of the Champions League's biggest competitors, and have some of Europe’s best young talent; both have Spanish head-coaches and sadly are both experiencing some major injury issues. Arsenal will be without Martin Odegaard, Mikel Merino, Oleksandr Zinchenko, Kieran Tierney and Takehiro Tomiyasu, who are all out with longer-term problems though some may push to return after the next international break.

arts.ttl: Bukayo Saka and Declan Rice to get unstoppable new Arsenal partner in latest Mikel Arteta boost - Tom Canton
arts.sum: As the team’s corner takers, the introduction of Mikel Merino to the team is sure to increase their chances of increasing their assist numbers – maybe one for Fantasy Football players to consider. Merino has already been spotted with the Arsenal team ahead of their game with PSG.
